---
applyTo: "db/migrate/**/*.rb,db/migrate_public/**/*.rb"
---

# Rails Migrations — WeDO Talent ATS

## Generation

ALWAYS use the Rails generator via Docker to avoid timestamp conflicts:

```bash
docker compose exec app rails g migration AddFieldToTable field_name:type
```

Never create migration files manually — the timestamp must be generated by Rails.

## Multi-tenancy

Migrations in `db/migrate/` run in ALL tenant schemas automatically via Apartment gem. Migrations for global/public-only tables go in `db/migrate_public/`.

- `db/migrate/` → tenant-scoped tables (jobs, candidates, applies, etc.)
- `db/migrate_public/` → public schema only (accounts, users, api_clients, etc.)

## Standard Pattern

Always use reversible `change` method:

```ruby
# frozen_string_literal: true

class AddPriorityToJobs < ActiveRecord::Migration[7.1]
  def change
    add_column :jobs, :priority, :integer, default: 0, null: false
    add_column :jobs, :urgency_level, :integer, default: 0, null: false
    add_index :jobs, :priority
  end
end
```

## Indexes

Add for every FK, every field used in `where`/`order`, and composite indexes for frequent queries:

```ruby
class AddIndexesToApplies < ActiveRecord::Migration[7.1]
  def change
    add_index :applies, :candidate_id
    add_index :applies, :job_id
    add_index :applies, :selective_process_id
    add_index :applies, [:job_id, :is_deleted, :created_at]
    add_index :applies, [:job_id, :selective_process_id, :is_deleted], name: "idx_applies_job_sp_deleted"
  end
end
```

## Column Types

```ruby
# Integers for enums
add_column :evaluations, :chatbot_channel, :integer, default: 0

# Boolean with default
add_column :jobs, :is_active, :boolean, default: false, null: false

# JSONB with default
add_column :sourcings, :parameters, :jsonb, default: {}

# Text for long content
add_column :jobs, :description, :text

# Decimal for money/scores
add_column :applies, :cv_match, :float
add_column :llm_usages, :cost_usd, :decimal, precision: 10, scale: 6

# Datetime
add_column :jobs, :published_date, :datetime
add_column :jobs, :closing_deadline, :date

# Array
add_column :jobs, :responsibilities, :string, array: true, default: []

# UUID
add_column :candidates, :uid, :string
add_index :candidates, :uid, unique: true
```

## Foreign Keys

```ruby
add_reference :applies, :candidate, foreign_key: true, null: false
add_reference :applies, :job, foreign_key: true, null: false
add_reference :jobs, :department, foreign_key: true, null: true
```

## Null Constraints

`null: false` for required fields. Always define defaults at the database level:

```ruby
add_column :jobs, :is_deleted, :boolean, default: false, null: false
add_column :evaluations, :time, :integer, default: 90
```

## Large Table Migrations

For tables with millions of rows, use `disable_ddl_transaction!`:

```ruby
class AddIndexToLargeTable < ActiveRecord::Migration[7.1]
  disable_ddl_transaction!

  def change
    add_index :applies, :created_at, algorithm: :concurrently
  end
end
```

## Rules

- **Always reversible** — use `change` method
- **Never mix schema and data migrations** — separate files
- **Indexes** for every FK and every field in WHERE/ORDER
- **Null constraints** for required fields
- **Defaults** at DB level, not just model level
- **Naming:** `AddFieldToTable`, `CreateTableName`, `RemoveFieldFromTable`
- **Generate via Docker** — never create manually
- **Test locally** before pushing — `docker compose exec app rails db:migrate`

## pgvector Extension

For embedding columns:

```ruby
class CreateEmbeddings < ActiveRecord::Migration[7.1]
  def change
    enable_extension "vector" unless extension_enabled?("vector")

    create_table :embeddings do |t|
      t.references :reference, polymorphic: true, null: false
      t.vector :embedding, limit: 768
      t.string :model_version
      t.timestamps
    end

    add_index :embeddings, [:reference_type, :reference_id], unique: true
  end
end
```
